hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'dixi' — 'Dico' is present indicative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'dicam' — 'Dico' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'dicam' — 'Dico' is present indicative

hints.duco, ducere, duxi, ductum: These are the parts of 'ducere' (to lead)
hints.dico, dicare, dicavi, dicatum: These are the parts of 'dicare' (to dedicate) — a 1st conjugation verb
hints.disco, discere, didici, —: These are the parts of 'discere' (to learn)

hints.accusative: 'Gaudium' as neuter can be accusative too, but here it is subject of 'erit'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'gaudii' ends in -ii/-i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'gaudii' ends in -ii
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'gaudii' ends in -ii

hints.to eat: 'To eat' is 'edere' — 'erit' is the future of 'esse'

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'est' — 'erit' with -it is future of 'esse'
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fuit' — 'erit' is future indicative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'erit' is future indicative

steps.reveal: in caelo = in heaven (abl. of place where)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'caelum' — 'caelo' with -o is ablative or dative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'caelum' — 'in' + ablative = location, not motion
hints.dative: 'Caelo' could be dative, but with 'in' it is ablative of place

steps.reveal: super uno peccatore = over one sinner (abl. with 'super')

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'unus peccator' — 'uno peccatore' is ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'unum peccatorem' — 'uno peccatore' is ablative
hints.dative: Dative would be 'uni peccatori' — 'uno peccatore' is ablative with 'super'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'peccatoris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'peccatoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'peccatoris' ends in -is

steps.target: ablative absolute (present participle + ablative noun phrase)
choices: ablative absolute (present participle + ablative noun phrase)

hints.ablative of means: Ablative of means has no participle — 'agente' is a participle forming an ablative absolute
hints.gerundive obligation: Gerundive would be 'agendum' — 'agente' is a present active participle
hints.accusative + infinitive: No infinitive is present — 'agente' is a participle in the ablative

hints.aio, —, —, —: These are the (defective) parts of 'aio' (to say yes/affirm) — different verb
hints.accipio, accipere, accepi, acceptum: These are the parts of 'accipere' (to receive)
hints.augeo, augere, auxi, auctum: These are the parts of 'augere' (to increase)

translation: I tell you that thus there will be joy in heaven over one sinner doing penance
